brief.title: Saturday, July 11 — six things creators need to know

items.html: Sora API sunsets September 24 — the app is already gone, so migrate any video pipeline dependencies now inside your 11-week window.
Meta launched Muse, a personal-photo image model, putting it directly against Adobe Firefly and Google Imagen for portraits, product shots, and room restyling.
OpenAI's new GPT-5.6 family ships in three tiers — Sol is 54% more token-efficient for coding, worth checking if AI-assisted writing or scripting is in your stack.
The NYT lawsuit escalated: publishers allege OpenAI concealed copyright-detection tools that could search training data and chat logs for protected content.
New ComfyUI tests show INT4 W4A4 quantization matches INT8 image quality at smaller file size — worth switching if VRAM is your bottleneck.
A free browser-based tool now auto-documents ComfyUI workflows from saved PNGs, extracting every custom node and model setting — fully offline.

tooldrop.name: Chiro Visuals Ivy & Fig Generator
tooldrop.blurb: Browser-based tool for 3D artists: paint paths on any mesh to grow procedural ivy or fig vines in real-time, no download needed.

stories.title: Artist transforms 2D pencil sketch into navigable 3D world
stories.dek: Leteramma scanned hand drawings, remapped paper as emission, and composed them into a traversable animation using Blender's geometry nodes. Specific technical execution with clear artistic intent.

stories.title: Meta kills Instagram AI feature allowing deepfakes of public accounts
stories.dek: Meta shut down a week-old tool that let users generate AI images by tagging public Instagram accounts without owner consent. The feature lacked notification and faced immediate backlash.

stories.title: Godot Road Generator adds procedural intersection lanes
stories.dek: TheDuckCow's open-source plugin now supports traffic lanes at intersections via community contribution. Includes customizable geometry, collision meshes, and AI lane pathfinding.
